objective: Layer deterministic, auditable, Resolution-aware Snapshot Memory through the Handbook Contract Membrane control pack and handoff/orchestration protocol without changing Rust.
work_completed: Separated memory horizons from memory record classes and defined Snapshot Memory as a cross-horizon record class.
Defined deterministic snapshot capture policy, strategic hooks, consistency modes, state/record fingerprints, redaction, retention, and immutable authority boundaries.
Defined deterministic SnapshotDelta and drift signal semantics with durable justification references.
Defined Resolution-aware snapshot projection so comprehensive capture does not imply comprehensive disclosure.
Added Snapshot Memory to target ownership, seam truth, Phase 3 sequencing, SDK/skill/Tauri posture, and proof gates.
Added optional snapshot refs to handoff records and integrated start/end snapshots, deltas, and grounding projections into orchestration and dispatch templates.

deliverables.summary: Expanded memory horizons/classes plus complete Snapshot Memory semantics, hooks, consistency, deltas, drift, projection, security, and handoff relationships.
Added preliminary capture-policy, ContextMemorySnapshot, SnapshotDelta, projection, redaction, retention, and SDK/evidence/gate contracts.
Added HCM-3.4 Snapshot Memory/delta engine and HCM-3.5 Resolution-aware adoption.
Added narrow live precursors and six Snapshot Memory proof gates plus handoff integration proof.
Added snapshot/delta selection, live start-state comparison, bounded grounding projection, and no-fabrication fallback.
Separated snapshot, handoff, and dispatch roles and added optional start/end/delta protocol.
Added backward-compatible optional snapshot reference object for new handoff records.

decisions.summary: Snapshot Memory is a memory record class spanning strategic, coordination, execution, and operation horizons, not a fifth horizon.
decisions.authority_effect: Memory taxonomy now separates horizon from record class.

decisions.summary: Snapshots are immutable descriptive evidence; contracts/artifacts remain normative truth and handoffs/dispatches remain transition authority.
decisions.authority_effect: Snapshot capture cannot lock, mutate, close, or rewrite canonical/transition truth.

decisions.summary: Snapshot deltas and drift signals are deterministic and cite durable justification; they do not invent causal intent.
decisions.authority_effect: Authorized divergence stays distinguishable from unexplained scope, proof, planning, or semantic drift.

decisions.summary: Agents receive Resolution-aware snapshot grounding projections rather than comprehensive snapshots by default.
decisions.authority_effect: Snapshot disclosure is constrained by scope, detail, authority, memory, and validation horizons.

findings.summary: Snapshot Memory is now fully represented in the control-pack target, but no runtime capture, delta, redaction, or projection implementation exists; all PG-SNAP gates remain open.
HCM-0.1 still requires the fresh independent control-pack review recorded by the superseded bootstrap handoff before implementation authority can freeze.

validation.command: jq parse plus Python Draft 2020-12 schema validation for handoff schema/template/prior records

pack_updates.reason: Make Snapshot Memory part of program scope and session grounding.
Preserve a concise pointer to snapshot/handoff/delta semantics in the architecture lineage.

next_session.objective: Select this superseding handoff and dispatch the fresh independent docs-only review of the complete HCM-0.1 control pack, including Snapshot Memory cohesion, contracts, security, orchestration, and proof gates.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh review checks Snapshot Memory taxonomy, authority separation, deterministic capture/delta semantics, consistency, redaction, retention, Resolution projection, and handoff integration.
Cross-document naming, HCM sequencing, schemas, and proof gates remain cohesive and concrete.
Actionable findings are remediated through a bounded dispatch and durable superseding handoff.
HCM-0.1 closes only after the complete control-pack proof gate is satisfied.
stop_conditions: Stop and record an escalation if review exposes an unresolved Snapshot Memory authority or privacy decision.
Do not begin Snapshot Memory or other Rust implementation before HCM-0.1 review closes.

chat_summary: Layered Snapshot Memory through the complete control pack: cross-horizon memory class, deterministic capture/deltas, drift signals, Resolution-aware projections, security, proof gates, orchestration, and optional handoff refs. HCM-0.1 remains review-required; no Rust changed.
